reviews.review_description:
Really enjoyable horror game set on a huge space ship. The level design is great with a variety of monsters and other hostiles. I have had no corridor fatigue as they keep you on your toes with combat, switching weapons and using your tools such as the blow torch, electric spanner and wire scanner all with nice feeling haptics implemented and climbing and crawling through vents. There is some decent object physics in the game. The voice work is quality and soundtrack very atmospheric, sound effects are on point with loud and subtle sounds, loved the 'Objective Updated' voice which may bring back memories and the mine boom reverberation never gets old, there are little sound effects like when picking up a datapad that all add to the quality feeling of the game. The smaller details are great as well, I always appreciate a washroom in VR where all the fittings work such as flush of toilet, taps, hand dryer and soap dispensers. I even came across one toilet cubicle unflushed with flies flying all around and large maggots wriggling around in its contents. I do not think I have ever seen that before, now that is detail haha.

The game surprised me with its quality all round, if you said to me the devs had made an AAA flat Sci-Fi horror game in past I would believe you. If the PSVR2 gets a version with headset and  Sense controller features implemented I will no doubt double dip.

First of all, the game is linear with loading screens which means that if you don't explore an area with loot, you don't get to go back. It also breaks the immersion. 
The atmosphere and the music is ok but gets better after some hours where memories come more often. I like the fact that it is not scary but there are many scare jumps.

The graphics are basic. The story is interesting but not very engaging. I wish we had more narration. After finding the "base", going back and forth with the train is not very interesting. The bar part is a bit weird (and at one point you rescue a guy and he just gives you a bottle which has nothing to do with the story). I would like a more scavenging-oriented gameplay with a free list of missions. The "riddles" are simple and ammo/credits are everywhere. The two cards needed for the computers are just near with no thinking or effort needed.

Overall it has arcade vibes : the same doors you used may be blocked and others that were blocked just open according to the story, not the actions (and I also found myself locked out and had to start all over). If you see the buying station, it's a safe place, if there is a turret, no other enemies and when you clear the path you just go along.

The physics are good but limited with climbing and shooting being quite clunky. Burning with the tool can also be complicated as you don't know if it really works or not. I had to replay the stage where I was running and the doors (that I used before) had bars because finding the right spot to burn needs more chance than anything (and as for the monster, you get to die because nothing tells you that it's coming).

The enemies change through the progression but they are not that challenging (the children however are ridiculously hard). 
The weapons are quite common and it would be better if we could carry more and even switch easier. Same for the grenades. I can only carry one and sometimes I even need to drop ammo to take one in. I don't see the utility of the mines as you only go forward and you cannot anticipate.

One thing that surprised me is the duration as it offers a lot of content for its price, even though it can be a bit repetitive. It doesn't get boring but it is not very engaging either and I don't think it offers much replayability.

Update: they just permanently slashed the price in half! You have no reason not to play this amazing game! Do yourself a favor and play this game!!!

This game is so good! How it didn’t get nominated for vr game of the year is beyond me. It’s so well balanced. The level design naturally flows you through the levels and still gives you a sence of exploration and always rewards you for your curiosity. There are tons of game mechanics that they keep throwing at you just before things start getting stale and never feels repetitive. It’s a pretty long game by vr standards.  Even if you don’t like your games on the scary side I still suggest you pick this one up. And its price tag is super low for what you’re getting. 

I would like to thank the devs for this labour of love. This is the vr game I’ve been waiting for.

reviews.review_title: A definite must play(*preferably on pc)
reviews.review_description:
Was not expecting all this, but when I heard that the team that did the Bioshock HLA remix made this game, I'm not surprised 

Great game for what they were working with, if someone were to give this team a AAA resource budget and they will produce. They know what they're doin

I’ve been VR gaming for over a decade now, this is a GREAT game. It’s fun to play, intriguing, and the visuals are sufficient bordering on great. This game is worth your time. I took off 1 star because the human enemy AI is dumb as a bag of bricks, but you don’t deal with them that often. I also wish this was developed only for Quest 3/3S and not Quest 2. That 7 year old chip is really a hinderance.

The game is great and has some amazing moments but it's also a bit inconsistent and it's not without flaws. For instance, the graphics: they vary from bad (like right at the start) to very impressive (especially at the second half of the game). The same can be said for the level design. A few sections were kind of meh, where you traverse generic looking corridors while killing a bunch of soldiers along the way, but other sections were simply amazing, with great graphics, eerie music, scary monsters, and an environment that feels like a scifi version of Propagation Paradise Hotel. The game is not without jank (like grabbing items, climbing ladders, and picking your tablet), but the gunplay of most weapons is decent and overall I thought the game had a lot more good parts than bad ones. The story is also surprisingly interesting and has some twists. Even with its flaws I think it deserves 5 stars as not only it has plenty of great moments, but the game is very ambitious and has a lot of content: lengthy campaign, good variety of enemies, multiple weapons that can be upgraded, different items that must be used in certain parts, boss fights, and an environment that's constantly changing as you progress in the story so it doesn't feel repetitive. I highly recommend the game for people who like these kinds of games.

If you are a fan of games like Bioshock or DEADSPACE you will love this one . Combat is a bit repatitive and dumb but game atmosphere and narrative beats overall . I spend somewhere around 15 hours to beat it

It takes a lot for a vr game to grab me the way this one did. The dread,tension, fear are top notch. I couldn't wait to get back on this game every time I had to take a break. A 11 to 13 hour campaign depending on your amount of exploration. Lots of secrets and reasons to find all the audio text and lore logs. Story is great and a wide variety of monsters to fight. My one small complaint it the enemy ai can be a bit dociel. Make the enemy's more aggressive. Once and a while the monster would not realize your there but that's he small complaint for a indie dev.

reviews.review_title: Just okay with some annoying controls
reviews.review_description: okay except when you push the left stick down to run, it is still too slow. No strafe. Belt holster when set to highest is still too low for someone who sits on a sofa. Cant reach the hypertab either on lower back while sitting.

reviews.review_title: Before the update 4 stars after 5 stars

reviews.review_description:
Initially the game didn't get many positive reviews but after two major updates it is definitely worth playing.
Pros:
 Graphics ( it's good for Quest 3)
Story: Simple but  interesting.
Combat is good  enough and th game have a decent variety of upgrades for your gun.
Music and sound effects  keep the atmosphere tense.
Cons:
Enemy Ali is pretty bad sometimes 
Graphical glitches here and there. Enemies walk and shoot through walls for example.
It's still an indie game and sometimes you will feel it more than others.
You only unlock extra difficulty after  beating the game so not much replayability at the moment (horde mode?)but it takes almost 12 hours to finish the game.
Overall it's a good game which looks like it's going to be supported for a while and it's a.definitely must buy for every fan of survival horror shooters.
Can't wait for Memoreum 2!

reviews.review_title: I had to give it a second shot
reviews.review_description:
I originally returned this game. I didn't like the way the enemy encounters worked. It felt too fast, the guns didn't seem to stumble the enemies at all, and I wasn't exactly having fun. And I'm still going to say the opening section of the game feels less engaging than the rest.

This one is about setting your expectations. Gameplay-wise, its pretty much the Doom 3 VR port by Team Beef, with fewer workarounds because it was made for this system. It also bears some resemblance to the recent Alien game. There are strong touches of Dead Space there too. The overall quality of it though, is closer to Genotype, which is a big indie compliment.

I'll give them this: The writing is better than expected, and I like the brutalist aesthetics of the ship, as well as the cyan/red color motif. But the best thing about it so far is the guns. They're just fun to use. Go in expecting something a little old school and it's really enjoyable. I dont regret buying it the second time.

It's better not to play this game if you have weak nerves.

In some places, the atmosphere is very scary.  

I love adrenaline and I like it.

I get a kick out of moments when you're afraid to move forward or afraid to put away your shotgun because someone might appear in the dark.

If you like that too, this game will give you exactly that feeling. Maybe even more.

It has a good storyline. It's interesting to move forward. 

The first 10 minutes are needed to get into it and understand what the game will be like. It may seem simple.

It's not. Then I found a backpack with a bunch of tools in it. You can unscrew bolts and take apart the walls of devices. There is crafting of supplies. There is also a gas burner.

It looks like there's a lot more interesting stuff to come.

I also saw a fight with a local boss. I liked that there was a hint in the notes that one of the women on the ship had a very vicious dog. And behind the door... guess who? 🤔😸 

I bought the game and don't regret it. Thank you to the creators for such a wonderful opportunity. This is exactly what was missing here, and it's probably one of the best games in this store.

I watched a couple of reviews before I purchased ￼and boy are they right ! Sometimes you don’t know if you should go and hide or just keep running. Even if nothing‘s going on in a room that you’re in just the music and sound effects. Keep you scared no matter where you are. I am extremely pleased with the  purchase. I only have about an hour and a half into the game and I can’t wait to get back home and get right back on.

reviews.review_title: New review since update - they fixed it!
reviews.review_description:
I had problems with the original version of this game but after the recent update it works fine.

So now, great game!  Seemed a bit simple at first but you soon find the game goes deeper than you expect.  Decent amount of gameplay, upgradeable weapons, tense vibes whilst playing but not too much.  Feels like a fun exploring adventure.

Highly recommended!

I'm in the minority her it seems, but I really tried to like this game. There's a lot going on. Good visuals, good sound, great atmosphere. Here's where it falls apart for me. I'll list 4 items you can pick up, healing hypo, ammo, crafting material, key cards. Of all of those items, there are 4 different ways to pick the up. What? Healing item, you have to take off your backpack and slot it into the proper area. Ammo, it goes to your shoulder opposite of your dominant hand. Crafting material, goes on your belt receptacle. Key cards, hold it in your hand and press A or X, depending on which hand it is in. That is, if the item doesn't fall on your feet instead of where it supposed to go. It's all so finicky. Why can't everything just go in the giant belt receptacle thing? If that doesn't bother you, then everything else is pretty good.

developer_response.body:
Hey Jeremy!

Sorry you were having problem with the controls. We did try to push the survival horror elements by making different systems for storage. One thing that might help you is to know you can actually store your ADI (the healing gun) in your front belt pouch as well for easier access than your backpack. Hopefully that helps a bit.

Thank you for the feedback and for playing!

reviews.review_description:
You can really tell the devs are pouring their hearts into this. The leap in graphics since day one is honestly staggering—it’s like a completely different game now.
​Everything just clicks. It’s spooky, packed with action, and the weapon variety (plus the upgrade system) keeps things feeling fresh. The music and sound effects are spot on for the atmosphere. It’s incredible that such a small indie team pulled this off. It feels like the perfect love child of Doom, Dead Space, and Alien.

This is a very ambitious title coming from a small team. It's lengthy, atmospheric and tense. A lot of love and care has been put into this experience and it really shows. Easily a top contender for best horror game on the Quest platform. Can't recommend it enough!

reviews.review_title: Otto Hudsons' daughter's heed is MASSIVE
reviews.review_description: I think she has a condition.

A very polished, atmospheric, and varied game with excellent controls and great weapon handling. It’s been refined to a mirror shine. Definitely one of the best titles on Quest. A must-play if you enjoy atmospheric shooters. And yes — there’s a gorgeous double-barreled shotgun.

Whatever I say about this game is a bit much. I see an amazing combination of Half Life, Doom and Alien that tie together wonderfully in this game. Its atmosphere is unique. The terror in the corridors is unbelievable. The long duration and plot of the game captivate you. Five stars are not enough for this gem.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review! We're so glad you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_description: Last patch improved graphics and bugs. Game is truly a roller coaster of insane scares, narrative, and lore. Will say not all bugs are gone, sometimes the enemies will stop attacking you and hand might get stuck onto non interactive items when picking up supplies

reviews.review_title: This is one of the good ones…
reviews.review_description:
This game slaps  -

I’d even say it’s better than Alien incursion in terms of atmosphere and horror. Especially because of the lighting effects and smoke. I wish aliens had that because it really makes a difference in the immersion. The voice acting is really well done. The graphics are fantastic. The monsters are scary as hell and taunt you as they chase you down. It’s an experience worth adding to your collection. Nothing else is like it that I’ve played.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review! We're so glad you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_description:
I don’t mean to poop all over the developers here but I just hate this less than mediocre game. I was intrigued and played a little too long so I couldn’t refund it, and boy does that feel like a waste of money. At least I played around 5 hours before deciding this game is making me waste time I’d otherwise spend on something else, like a better game. 
The graphics are bad and not in a nostalgic way.
The rooms are all tiny and claustrophobic and depressing.
The map system is lacking. You’re not on there for starters.
The enemies are repetitive.
There are unclear objectives at times that you don’t know where to go.
Lord how I hate I’ve wasted my money on this. You want a similar game that rules, try Shock Troopers.

7hours in and my save file when missing,... So played in hard mode since got a hang of the gane....then disaster struck again at 15 hr mark when got the hikari final up grade...then the last door just wont open for me to progress the story.. Then it really when to hell..as i reload uninstalled and replayed that part..random enemies previously killed just decided to get reincarnated randomly behind walls or just respawn randomly as i thought i missed a puzzle or etc. Usually there a reporting link for these type of problems where i can just upload gameplay videos or photos...so decided to report here instead..meanwhile getting a fix i will play a new game again..just to get to the ending.
at this point im a speedrunner for this game...please developers patch this glitch. I really want to mk.4 that robo so bad.        
21st february 26: 
now im giving them 5 Stars!!! the latest patch solved my problem. Sadly i wasnt a patient man. I saved up the past 2 months to get my self a quest 3...low and behold i manage to proceed  through the stuck door and finally dismantled that robo bast**rd. Tips: you need a screwdiver to k*ll it. Dont be like me fraging him with six grenades and scrathing my head why his "knocked" animation made him f***ng immortal!. The developers got me this time. The jump from quest2 to quest3 has made me. Shed tears of joy. The moment i can see the gory decals and shiny space decks. Cant wait for the sequel!!! this game really scratched my itch of deadspace + the old shooter game "fear 1&2" .dont take my review lightly guys. Take it from a guy that spend 100++ hours. Of trying to brute force my way through a glitch.just to unscrew the back end of a b**tard robo.

I wasn't ready for what this game had in store! I originally bought it thinking it was something like the game Cosmodread if you know what that is. Basically a game where you kill aliens on a ship and try to escape but its less focused on the story and more on shooting and survival. Again, I bought this game thinking it was another thing like that but I was honestly suprised. Not only was the story more intreaguing and creative than most vr games but the play time as almost double the usual average for my other story games! I really loved it and I am glad I got to paly it as it was a work of art. I may be a Sci-fi nerd but this game honestly deserves my respect. A WORK OF ART!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review! We're so glad you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_title: Doom 3 meets Dead Space corridor shooter
reviews.review_description:
Edit: version 1.4 addressed the majority of my complaints and the game is quite polished and well-rounded now. I can give it a strong recommendation. There are still some lingering issues (ragdolls, weapon handling, map design) but it's a major improvement over what it was when I reviewed it. Now is the time to jump in!
----------------------------------------------------------

This game is a bit of a rollercoaster in terms of quality and polish. Underneath, there's a decent, traditional, corridor shooter and spooky adventure game that wears its influences on its sleeve (everything from Doom 3 to Dead Space to the movie titled The Ring). There's also a lot of everything: levels, enemy types, weapons, lines of dialogue, boss fights, you name it.

Getting between you and all that, though, are quite a few issues. The developers are hard at work on the bugs with regular updates but there are other issues, like combat balancing, item placement and frequency, level design, and texture work. Overall, the game feels uneven and the combat is too easy once you have some basic mastery over the core mechanics and game loop (I found Hard mode to be quite easy). 

Basically there are a lot of sections in the game where everything comes together and it shines, and other parts that feel like filler or like they're lacking in polish. 

I don't want to be too negative, however,  since the devs were ambitious and aimed much higher than other small teams do with the sheer amount of everything in the game, I just hope they can polish and refine it further (if I see it gets regular updates, I may play through it again in a year or two and update this review). 

I should also add that I play through most of these types of Quest games so my standards are probably pretty high by now. I do recommend it and hope that it sees steady improvement.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for jumping back in! Glad you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_description:
Fun spooky spaceship game with bouts of panic from the get-go. Good visuals on the Quest 2 and fab sound design. I love to dual wield the zappy starter gun a shotgun. 

I had a technical issue after a reloaded save game and, after reporting it on Discord, the devs responded and fixed it in the next update.  Keep an eye out for vents.

I would like an "always run" option as it is very easy to accidentally toggle  between walk slowly and run whilst having a panic.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review and the feedback! We're so glad you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_description:
Second Update: Confirmed that the booze bottle bug still exists in that the first four bottles are already on the shelf , so I can’t unload the first two bottles I’ve collected out of my pack (and obviously can’t get “paid” for them. This will be frustrating for first time players since they’ll think they’re doing something wrong.
Update: I was actually replaying through the game, mid-update and was already past the part where they explain the lock scanning (which seems to be a new thing in the update) I’m curious if I run into the bottle collection issue… How do I contact the devs for bug reports. I want to like the game but… Restarted the game in a new slot after the update, and once I get to the junction/bar, there are four liquor bottles already populated, so I can’t get rid of the two in my pack. Got to the prison (that I already got past the first slot attempt), and the spot where you use the electric detector, it shows a lock on the wall, (by the red shielded goodies on the shelves) but nothing to do here. The door forward is Orange, and when I go back through the door I came in, it shuts and then goes from blue to orange, and then I’m just stuck. I’m removing the game and attempting a fresh install.

Update: I was able to finish the game last night. Took me about 17 hours. At first it seemed a bit repetitive: walk the halls and check the rooms, shoot things. I thought after the first section that would be the end of the game, but it kept going and I think got better, introducing different elements. I enjoyed it and think it’s good for the price. 
1st review: So I’m about 15 hours in and at the labs section. I got the secret weapon from the office and I’m trying to get to the last U-shaped section of halls. The door is blue but won’t open so it seems like I can’t progress any further. I’ve been exiting the game properly. Please help.
Not the best horror game on the quest, but definitely worth the money.

Since release I’ve been living inside of this game and it got even better with every update.
Fantastic story, absolute stunning graphics, very atmospheric game design and a great soundtrack as well as a high replay value.
I had an absolute blast with it.

reviews.review_title: One of the best quest games
reviews.review_description: Love, Love this game. One of the best on the quest. Story, graphics, atmosphere, great inventory and gun management. It has it all and a lengthy campaign as well. Just a small team of developers so kudos to them.
Great horror shooter with a solid story.  The mechanics are well done.  Sound and graphics are both great. This deserves a spot in your library for sure.  Great job Dev's!!

A mix of half life, the thing and alien isolation. 
It's been years I played a well done story based fps. .
Not a single glitch. Perfect gameplay.
This is a very good game

reviews.review_title: A must play for horror fans
reviews.review_description:
This game is amazing, I don't want to spoil anything, so I'll keep it short. 
If you want to compare it with a flat screen game you could say it's like Dead Space 2, but it really has an identity of it's own. The entire game is full of creepy scenarios, getting creepier as the game goes on. The darkness and sound effects makes every step feel like you can get jumped at any time.
The story is very interesting and keeps you wondering what's going on though the entire playtrough, and every area has it own essence that makes you understand what happened before you came in.
If you saw some reviews on youtube from the biggest creators, I can assure that most of the complaints were fixed, the game got almost 5-6 updates while I played it.
You can expect 13hs of gameplay if you explore everything, so I think it's a great deal, specially if you play agaun wih different weapons.
SLIGHT GAMEPLAY SPOILERS
The gameplay is really good, every weapon is fun, but once you upgrade your weapons you can just use it through the entire game because ammo is not scarce at all, you can be shooting everything with your upgraded weapon and it won't be necessary to save ammo even on hard difficulty, I think it would be a better experienceif ammo were way scarcer, I felt like there was no need to use the forge during my playtrough. I didn't play on Black Hand difficulty yet but I hope it gets way harder.
A good way to make the game harder could be to increase the enemy's speed, it was pretty easy to avoid them if you are careful, but I died like 10 times during my playtrough so it wasn't super easy.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review and the feedback! We're so glad you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_description:
Great game, from intuitive shooting mechanics to scary and interesting story.  Very well produced and fun gameplay.  

Occasionally a little stimulated as to how to progress and sometimes accidentally finish a level rather than exploring as it wasn’t clear which way was the end level but other than that great fun, with elements reminiscent of playing Doom or Quake for the first time.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review! We're so glad you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_title: Think Doom 3, The Thing & The Ring......
reviews.review_description:
*Update - 9th February *
Finally, I resolved the issue. Had a few exchanges with the devs on Discord and uploaded several videos. OK, so I'll relay the issue to other users who may be interested without giving too much of the game plot away. On the 'Surface Base' level, you're instructed to look out the 'Terraforming observation window' by an NPC. I was supposed to see something in the lightning storm that then triggers the next sequence. For whatever reason, I just wasn't quite hitting the observation trigger spot even though the devs told me exactly what to look out for and what should happen next. On my final attempt, I pressed my mug right up to the VR glass and stared into the storm, focusing on one spot. That finally did the trick.

I did ask the devs if I was doing something wrong or just a bit unlucky. They surmised that I may have been unlucky but they deduced that I wasn't quite hitting the spot on the look trigger. This was quite frustrating for a while but big thanks to the devs for taking the time to reply on here and responding quite quickly on Discord a few times until the issue was resolved. I'll edit my review accordingly.

------------------------------

*Update - 8th February*
As requested, I've joined your Discord server and uploaded a video that will hopefully illustrate clearly what I was trying to say below for the game breaking bug. I even uploaded a second video as someone tried to correct me and say I should look out the window and see a cutscene. Hopefully, this helps the dev team.

----------------------------

*Edit: Response to devs*
Thanks for your quick response. Unfortunately, it is 100% a bug. Before I submitted my review, I made a point of scrolling through a few other reviews to see if anyone else who had issues had encountered the same bug. A Google search also didn't yield any similar experiences from other players. As you'll note from my original review, I can turn, crouch and move my hands but I can't move forwards, backwards or sideways using the thumbtacks once I'm within about 2-3 feet of the observation window. Believe me, I tried many times.

The volume level on my headset is absolutely fine and there is next to zero background noise in my playing environment. I didn't hear any giggling that you mentioned. It was blatantly obvious to me that something was supposed to happen at point in the game plot and I wasn't just supposed to stare out of the window. I'm fully cognisant that some players will leave leave 1 star reviews for some games because they merely get stuck on a puzzle or the game becomes too hard. I can assure you that I don't fall into that category. I also suspect as I've eluded to that this bug hasn't been encountered before. One thing that may be of note is that I saved the game a few days ago right at the start of the surface base level to return to it yesterday. I've noticed some players have issues when they've saved progress and tried to return to the game at a later time.

I've noticed that on the game profile, you can only reload from one previous checkpoint; please correct me if I'm wrong. If I'd had the option, I would have tried to reload from an earlier checkpoint before that level to see if I could fix the problem. I think the option to reload from the start of any previous level would be great. There was at least one level where I didn't get the chance to explore one small section of the map because I suddenly found that I'd completed that level. I realised that I'd have to play from the very start of the game again after several hours, just to explore the small section I'd missed. I noticed that someone else online had said a similar thing.

Guys, I do not want to bash the work you've done just for the sake of it but as devs and clearly folk who love games, I'm sure you can understand my frustration after spending around 15 hours on this game exploring every little facet. If a simple reinstall had fixed the problem, I'd say that was forgiveable. Maybe I'll have to restart from the very beginning again at some point on a new profile and hope for the best; understandably, I really didn't feel like doing that last night.

I'll give you kudos where it's richly deserved as follows:
- The story is A*, and I was 100% invested. On a couple of occasions with this game, "Just 5 more minutes..." turned into an hour and that's not hyperbole. That's how much I was into the story.
- Ability to interact with random objects and throw them around. Might seem trivial but to me and many others, it adds a layer of immersion.
- The enemies look great are varied and some feel genuinely menacing. Your fallen foes don't magically evaporate as you see in many other games. They lay scattered around the map.
- Gun mechanics feel solid and I like the upgrade system. This doesn't feel like Doom 2 having a plethora of weapons and what feels like infinite ammo. I found myself relying on my blaster when possible to conserve ammo for my main weapon when things got ropey.
- Atmosphere, sound effects, voice acting and game environment are solid. I like the ragdoll physics on some occasions such as the dead body floating around in zero gravity. I like the little humorous elements you come across now and again despite the sense of dread. Yes, I found the film poster for the buddy cop story; I'll let others find that gem and won't spoil the surprise. 
- Rucksack and tool kit are well thought out. You have very limited storage slots for ammo, medkits and grenades so you can't hoard everything and ultimately become unstoppable.

I'll upgrade my review from a 2 to a 3 star on reflection right now but as you'll appreciate, I can't give it the 4 or 5 right now that I felt it was in line for based on what I'd played so far before the game breaking bug.

----------‐--------‐--‐---------------

A real shame. As my heading states, I encountered a game breaking bug today which stopped me from progressing any further. I'll detail as follows, should the devs wish to take note. On the surface base level, shortly after waking up in the med lab, I'm advised to go over to the terraforming observation window. Every time I do and I'm close to the window after the shutters open, my feet end up glued to the spot. I can turn, crouch and move my hands around but I can not move from the spot. I wait and wait.... and nothing happens. I tried reloading the checkpoint several times but the exact same thing happens every time. I even uninstalled the game and then reinstalled it... but encountered the exact same problem. Feet glued to the spot after approaching the window.

What's really disappointing is that this was really shaping up to be quite a good game with a strong, interesting story. This game was released on 09 October, 2025. I also held off from buying the game initially. Firstly, for any major bugs to be ironed out and secondly for any specific Q3 updates. I had noticed that a few players had encountered game breaking bugs of various different types but many reviews were positive; seemingly from those who had not encountered any major issues. I must have bought about 30 games  in total for the Q3 since launch, 90% of which I've completed. Previously, I've only ever had to reinstall one game which refused to load after an update. The reinstall fixed the problem and I kept my saved game data. Sure, I've had the occasional software crash with other games but never a game breaking bug like this that halts all progress completely. It's a real disappointment to say the least.

developer_response.body: Glad we got it figured out! Enjoy the rest of the game!

reviews.review_description: I have no idea how much further I have to go in this supremely gripping game, which has for me one of the best and well-crafted stories of any standalone VR game to date. I'm currently in the 'Labs' section of the game (15 hours in, although I do play very slowly and love to explore all the nooks and crannies, which at times is essential, or you'll miss vital information) and I'm still encountering new varieties of enemies. So far I've only really experienced one issue, but after a couple of checkpoint reloads it sorted itself out. Everything is in place for this game to become one of the 'Must Buy' games for standalone VR. Yes I guess it still needs a little polish here and there, for instance I cannot for the life of me work out how to store grenades in the backpack, but this is for me a minor issue that I'm confident will be smoothed out over time, but the thing I'm most impressed with is the story and the absolutely amazing voice-acting, the emotion in the line-delivery is genuinely perfect and everyone associated with that should receive all the plaudits for their exceptional work. You might think that it's just another 'character fights infection in deep space' game, but the story has some wonderful twists and turns that have me second guessing myself quite often. If as many VR creators state 'Saints & Sinners' is a must own VR game, then this easily ranks alongside that.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review and the feedback! We're so glad you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_description:
This in one of the best games that I have ever played on the Oculus system. 
Great story, great weapons, and awesome combat.

The graphics and art style are phenomenal. Everything looks super cool. The gunplay is fun, but I have personally played better vr gun games. The enemies have a very basic AI but I am sure it will improve with upcoming updates. The non-gunplay mechanics are also pretty fun to do (I especially like the calibration tablet thing with the two knobs). A setting to make the flashlight togglable rather than automatic would be nice. I am really enjoying the story so far, and it seems like I am far from finishing the game which is a good thing because I like playing the game. I did get softlocked at around hour 8 but I just had to restart my save and speedrun the game back to the point I left off at. I hope that this game recieves enough support and revenue to make either a prequel or sequel because that would be awesome

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the review and the feedback! We're so glad you enjoyed it!

If you ever encounter any more soft-locks, we added chapter select in the newest update that should fix anymore of those issues.

reviews.review_description:
One of the best looking game on quest 3.
I finish the game on hard mode.
The immersion is good and the story too.
The only one small problem I got was the weapons that are unloaded ramdomly and the Ikari who refuse to shoot after some load screen, to fix it I need to restart at the last check point.
Can't wait to see how the pc version will perform because I will buy it again.
Thanks for the team who gave us a game I want to replay again.

developer_response.body: heeey thanks!! that gun isseu wil be resolved when we launch the update soon. and also working on pc version!

reviews.review_description: 3 hours in and loving it. Loved Dead Space and Half Life. Thank you developers. More please !

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review! We're so glad you're enjoying it!

reviews.review_title: Great game, got past my softlock
reviews.review_description:
i've really been enjoying it so far. Really worth it for the experience it brings. But i've run into a snag that halts my 8 hour progression.

I loaded into a section of the game where you hand an essential item to a quest giver and turned off the Quest 3 for the night. Now when i load back in i start at the beginning of the instance/mission, I no longer have the essential item, softlocking me out of game progression. Kind of a bummer since i really was enjoying the game. if your on the fence about getting it, I Can say i was having a blast for pretty much the whole time.

edit: the game is good enough that i just started a new game and played past the part i was softlocked at. Worth it ;)

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the review! We're so glad you enjoyed it!

if anything like that ever happens again, we have added chapter select so you won't lose progress!

reviews.review_description:
Game runs smooth graphics look stunning and character models are designed so well and creepy. Many many different enemies and weapons to choose from with an amazing story

Besides minor bugs like ammo not saving through loading screens or projectiles sometimes hitting you through walls, I’ve run into little to no issues with the game. At $30 the game is definitely one of the best and this price range and just in general.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review! We're so glad you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_description: Great job love the update you guys rock keep it up

Aside from Into the Radius 2 Early Access this is the most surprising and impressive game I’ve seen in a while.  
First off this game looks sleek and gorgeous. Especially with Quest Games Optomizer.  The trailers for this game do not come close to showcasing the glorious immersion of this  “Top Shelf” experience.
Furthermore for $19 this  is a game that could easily go for $29.99.  To
🤯👌👌👏👏👏👏👏bravo! 
Highly recommended checking out this superb game.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review! We're so glad you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_title: Finally a spiritual dead space successor
reviews.review_description:
I'm relatively early in this game but this is such a well made title.
Graphics on quest 3 look ridiculously good with lighting and reflections.
Atmosphere is super creepy with small clues here and there that further suck you into its world. I find myself exploring and looking at everything, which I never do unless a vr game is of exceptional quality. I am yet to find a storage box that does not show "corrupted" when I press on the button.
I wonder if it would be possible to add real time reflections from the guns, so it lights up a little bit on the walls when the shots fly past them?
Hope this game sells well because you've earned it for sure devs!

developer_response.body: thanks you for the kind words!! dynamic lights are fps killers on quest standalone devices cant do that :d

reviews.review_description: So if you like space and you like horror and you love putting them together, this game is fantastic. I cannot stop playing this game since I started it. Great job to the developers. A lot of effort has been put into this game and it shows! Beautiful graphics, great movement, awesome combat, everything just feels right. One of my new favorites!!!!!

This is like Doom, alien and other sci-fi space shooter with great adventure elements. So many different rooms to explore and much to do! 5 star rating is not enough for this masterpiece one of best vr adventure shooter in space!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review! We're so glad you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_description: Hello so I think this game is good details story and all but I’m stuck on a area that needs a passcode at the beginning and need to pull out my tablet this will sounds dumb but I can’t test due to health problems I can’t stand the sit feature in the setting I was still unable to grab my tablet is there a solution to this? Sorry again great game I just can’t progress and am stuck hopefully a developer sees this

developer_response.body:
Hey there Hazel! Not a silly thing at all, we take accessibility very seriously and do everything we can as an indie team.

So we do test seated a lot of time. When you reach for your lower back while seated and press and hold the grip button, you should be able to remove your tablet. If you have any more trouble at all, please feel free to reach out on here or our Discord, and we will do everything we can!

discord.gg/NcnB5BV2EE

reviews.review_description: Great horror and atmosphere that will keep you on your toes. There are bugs that can and will be fixed because the devs care and respond. Here’s my grievances: to those posting about the save deletions, don’t take off headset or turn off headset while the save icon is showing, this hasn’t happened to me since abiding by this rule. Also upon restarting checkpoint I noticed the path I already took ammo and credits respawned and allow the player to cheat and get more resources than the developers intended.  Also I just used the shotguns because with other weapons spare ammo left in magazine disappears except shotguns on reload which is bad for a survival resource focused game, I hope this gets fixed because the snipers are so cool and one shot headshot the soldiers.

developer_response.body: this wil 100 percent be fixt soon :d thanks for playing!!

reviews.review_description: This game needs more popularity very enjoyable. It felt like there was some psychological horror in parts. Over all very good game

A few hours in... now.. just want to express something about some 5 star reviewers... they almost all got no followers and only been made for this game review.
That being said, also if you want to contact the staff trough their email(website) and give some feedback it wont work.. the mail adress doesnt exist. 

Now i like the game for what it is and im gonna play it all for sure. But the dev's know this must be quest 3 updated! Otherwise its a bad look for Quest3 user who payed alot more money the see higher fidelity games. Even with ultra settings on Quest Game Optimizer it looks rough. Textures needs to be higher quality and the blurryness and the jagged edges needs to get smoothed out. ( there are tools for the dev's to do this easily )

***********
**UPDATE**
***********


The Dev's kept their word and the update they put out blows my mind! This is absolutely the change it needed. Fully sharp vision with higher resolution! Better lighting and more creepy looking enemies.
Packed with the awesome atmosphere and creepy vibes I cannot put this game down and have to see whats next everytime!! Great job !!!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for updating your review and for giving the game another chance — we really appreciate it!  It’s awesome to hear that the latest update made such a big difference for you and that you’re now fully enjoying the atmosphere, visuals, and those extra-creepy enemies. 

The team worked incredibly hard on improving the resolution, lighting, and overall fidelity for Quest 3, so it’s amazing to know the results hit the mark. Thank you for recognizing that effort and for sticking with us — feedback like yours keeps us motivated to keep pushing the experience even further.

reviews.review_title: Great game, slightly held back by bugs
reviews.review_description:
This is a great game. The comparisons to Dead Space are obvious, but I also got some inspiration from games like F.E.A.R. And Resident Evil 4. This will probably be pretty long, so if you just want to know if it’s a good game or not, the answer is, “yes, but-“. Yes, it’s a great game, but where it suffers a little are several bugs that I encountered.

 I had a lot of issues with collectibles in the game. There are audio and text logs you find throughout the ship, and some of those logs  help you open special lockers in the hub. But try as I might, I cannot access any of them. I click on them, and the screen doesn’t change. 

I also had an issue where I found one of the side quest items, turned it in, and got my reward (a new pew-pew) and then had to go pick up dinner (in real life) so I shut the game off. Well, the game checkpointed me after depositing the item, but without giving me the weapon. I had several other issues with shutting the game off and returning. 

Probably the most annoying issue is de-spawning ammo. When you travel from one area to another, all of your guns are un-loaded. In a game where you often have a split second before being set upon by horrors, it’s a bit of a downer. Other times your other weapons are loaded with the absolute wrong ammo (think a machine gun with shotgun shells floating mysteriously where the mag is supposed to be). 

Also, some of the charging handles (how you reload the weapon) is right where the secondary grip is. This is especially bad on the highest-level bolt-action rifle. Instead of pulling the bolt back to fire another round, you wind up just gripping the gun. Conversely, you go to stabilize the gun and wind up ejecting a cartridge onto the floor. Again, not great if you need to fire quickly. 

I’ve looked at the patch notes, and several of the issues I mentioned are listed as “fixed”. So I tried updating, and it’s saying it’s up to date, so… I dunno. Now, none of these things were a dealbreaker for me. Nor do I think they will be for most people. But this is definitely worth a play. I was honestly expecting Temu Dead Space, but it was pretty solid all around. Couple of graphical gripes here and there (the hub area after a certain event happens really stands out). But the atmosphere is suitably tense. Lots of ambient sound that makes you jump. Doors that open to pitch black, and your immediate thought is, “I don’t wanna go in there” is a good indicator. There were several times I had to quit the game just because the atmosphere was so tense, and I needed something “lighter”for a bit. Was I scared? No, not really, but I’m kind of a horror junkie, so your mileage may vary. The game took me about 15 hours to complete, but I investigate quite a bit, even more so in VR to see all the weird objects I can pick up and throw. All in all, I enjoyed my time with this game, and I’ll likely return to it at some point, ideally once the above issues are resolved.

developer_response.body: working on it !

reviews.review_description:
Graphics look nice and I enjoy the jump scares. In my opinion so far it’s not as good as Propagation: Paradise Hotel but I’ve tried multiple games and this is probably the closest thing to it and it does seem to be getting better. I think the enemies talking does ruin the vibe a little. I was having trouble loading the shotgun because I assumed that I was supposed to load the bullets into the hole that opens up in the upper part of the shotgun and it turns out that they’re loaded in the bottom. I think this game does feel like a high quality game and I recommend it to anyone that enjoys survival horror games or sci-fi like Dead Space.

Edit: I decided to lower my review from 5 to 3 stars due to some issues with the shotgun sometimes you can shoot one bullet then you have to pump the shotgun and sometimes you can shoot 3 before having to pump again. I died a few times because I thought I would have to pump it again and once because I thought I could shoot again without pumping it, it’s too random. There also seems to be a glitch that doesn’t let me take bullets out of the box on my hip when I know I still have ammo in there because I just put some in there. Taking out and holding the shotgun with two hands is also a challenge, I swear sometimes I reach for it on my right shoulder and it pulls out the backpack. Most of the time my left hand won’t grab onto the shotgun then I end up dying. I hope they fix these issues.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the review! We're so glad you enjoyed it!

As for the bugs, we just released our newest update that should fix some of the issues you were experiencing.

reviews.review_description: Just rolled credits. You can probably rush through in around 8-10 hours but I took my time checking every room, closet, corner and door, so was a shade over 14 hours. In just three days as I played every spare minute I had. Don’t think twice. Buy it. I’d play it again right now but I’m waiting for the PSVR2 port as I think that will be even better with the extra features that platform brings to horror. But Quest gamers… don’t think twice. Buy it now, and put on some old underwear you don’t care about while it’s downloading. ;-)

developer_response.body:
thank you so much for playing all the way through and for such an amazing review! It means a lot that you took the time to explore every corner and still came out excited and recommending the game. Hearing that you spent 14 hours in it over just three days is honestly incredible.

We’re thrilled you enjoyed the experience, and the PSVR2 comment made our day we agree that platform brings some great opportunities for even deeper immersion.

Seriously, thank you for the support and the laughs. Feedback like this keeps us motivated!

reviews.review_description:
This game once I saw the infection all over the place and the shooting pod spiders reminded me and felt like a higher end knockoff  of “By Grit Alone” which is definitely not for younger viewers. It feels like Dead Space and Alien Rogue Incursion were merged together to make this “EARLY ACCESS” game. I’m not entirely sure why but personally I feel underwhelmed. The gore element and monster grotesque is there but I find myself only really flinching at the shadow individual with red eyes who proceeds the girl every so often. They should have taken from A.R.I. And had the deformed undead colonizers actually hunt you down rather than you going into a room and shooting the enemy and then the threat is dealt with. It takes away from the fear factory because nothing will show up behind you or come out of an air duct you already cleared. The amount of shotgun shells or ammo clips once you reach the gun modification fabricator also makes it feel much less stressful since you always have to much ammo to use while I found myself only using the pistol just to add an extra level of difficultly; I’m playing on normal mode or intermediate for reference. I wish you could choose where to move the ammo bag because the reaching and bending of the left shoulder and arm feels unnatural and actually quite difficult. I sometimes have to look down just to see it.  The environment also lacks the ability to fully interact with it. Theirs things you can move obviously but so much that you can’t. Like in by grit alone everytime you step on the fleshly walls nd remains theirs this awful squishing noise this game lacks that for the most part. And when the environment is to dark it automatically turns the light on for you I wish you had to manually do it or even run out of juice like TWD saints and sinners. In every loading screen it also resets your main so make sure to relock and load each time before proceeding and having to pull the slide or pump every time you take the main back out is ridiculous or if I accidentally drop a clip it disappears and counts as a full mag atleast for me. Just all these little things that add up make it uninspiring and a meh. The story is good though and I’m interested but I feel underwhelmed and unchallenged. And the running mechanics is awful so that boss fight was purely me just getting bit down like a rag doll healing and shooting the armor off. It’s early access and it shows from your guns and hands disappearing in the walls and environment to mag dumping an enemy even after shooting all their pod weak points. I gave it a four though because the gore for standalone is still commendable for how it draws you into the oh boy atmosphere and actually being able to move around those certain things pertaining to but theirs still much improvement to be done.  And I preordered it but only my pistol is golden. Perhaps it’s the final customization of some weapons that’s actually golden. We shall see I suppose.

Update

The recent update that made the game go from 10gb to 20 addresses many graphical bugs and also fixed most of the problems I was experiencing. The  textures and even the environment are much more affected by the virus and it even moves and grows as you pass it. Along with the enemy actually spawning out more even after clearing a room that you return to. Much better. I think it should have just dropped like that as games even when in early access either grabs the player with its first impression to stay and play through or to give it back. I have a 512 gb meta quest 3 and am more than willing to clear up space for a top tier campaign game. Well done devs keep it up. Only thing I will say, the boss chase with the robot on the train was really rough took me a second to find the door and every other place I was able to access had no floor to it and I infinitely fell haha

Finished the game:

Peak, peak, peak. No spoilers but that is ridiculous. Love it

developer_response.body: Haha we are so glad you stuck with it, and thank you so much for the kind words and the feedback too. We're gonna do everything we can to keep improving MEMOREUM!

Hey Diz, sorry you ran into that!

We are really trying to figure out the issue you are encountering. Would you mind joining our discord and maybe uploading a video of what you are experiencing? We have checked Monty Co Correctional multiple times but found no progression blockers on our end and were able to beat it.

Looking forward to hearing from you:

reviews.review_description:
Once i finished the game i can say it's one of the best No PCVR game so far. At the beginning the game looked to me normal but the more i was going forward, the more i liked. Looking forward for a 2nd part

PROS
+ Good graphics for a standalone game (no PCVR) + Hand collitions with scenary + Great ambience + It's not only about shoot you also need to use items + Fun and easy to play + Mix of Dead Space and Half Life + Long game (more than 13h) + Different bosses + It becomes better and better
 
CONS
- The enemies run too fast towards you and it's difficult escape trying to recharge the weapon - Manage inventary and bullets is a bit mess + Near to the end, in multiple vision enemies you die frecuently and have to start far back until you discover what enemy is the real

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much, both for your feedback and your support! We're gonna do everything we can to keep improving MEMOREUM.

We can say, for the Sirens, aim for the copy that has the glitch effect around their head!

reviews.review_description: UPDATE. Thanks for the help. I will try again. Maybe it would be a good idea to put a message saying press X to use keycard or better still put it in your backpack or somewhere a bit more obvious. AUTO SAVING  is still an issue. ORIGINAL: I thought this was great for the first hour till the first boss. It's not hard and I have beaten several times but I am unable to progress. I've had enough. First time I stopped after getting the collar only to find it didn't auto- save the progress. I had to fight it again and again (BTW it's almost impossible to reload sat down). Then I beat it again, get the keycard, move on to the next section and the card isn't anywhere, not in my backpack - I can't use it to open the door. I reload the checkpoint to see if it comes back and guess what - I'm back at the boss fight! DEVS - WHEN YOU KILL A BOSS YOU SHOULD AUTO-SAVE. I think every game ever created does this!

developer_response.body: press x onleft controller it wil show you the keycard that you have collected where you put coller from dog.

reviews.review_description: I mean just top tier and continues to develop into an amazing game! Gonna be one of them I end up replaying again cause of how good it is. Resident evil style game for sure

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review! We're so glad you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_description:
Started playing today. Right after everything turns red and you drop down from vent in that room is my glitch area.
I saw a video where your supposed to unscrew and I do but my end of game there’s no blue gen to shoot so I can proceed outside of room. 
Enjoying my experience but I can proceed.

developer_response.body: heey!! can you contact uss on dicord memoreum? would love to help you out

reviews.review_description: It happens on quest3

developer_response.body:
Hey there!

Sorry to hear you are stuck. Would you mind letting us know where you are stuck in the Correctional Facility? We would be happy to help! Feel free to update your review with those details or you can join our Discord for a direct line: discord.gg/NcnB5BV2EE

reviews.review_description:
(The file save wiping issue appears to be fixed by the dev team)

A must-own title for fans of horror, sci fi, and shooters. 

Graphics: clean resolution, majority of textures are sharp, reflective surfaces, smoke/fog/steam/fire/light/shadow effects are all incredibly impressive on a standalone headset (Quest 3). Minimal frame hitches. 

Sound: music and dialogue are clean, clear, and add to the atmosphere and story very well. Voice acting is believable and keeps you immersed. 

Gameplay: handling weapons and exploring the large vessel is immersive and tense. The story is paced well, with the mystery unfolding at an even pace with the game progression.  Environmental puzzles utilize all your tools and climbing abilities. Enemy variety is welcome, with their tactics becoming more deadly as the game progresses.

developer_response.body:
I’m really sorry for the frustration this has caused. We’re aware of the save-data issue and the impact it’s having, and our team is working around the clock to get it fixed as quickly as possible. The workaround the developers provided is only temporary, and we know it isn’t idealespecially if you’re spawning in without weapons.

We appreciate your patience, and we’re doing everything we can to restore the game experience you were enjoying. Thank you for sticking with us.we wil add a chapter select soon

reviews.review_description: I’m m 2 hours in and am excited to see where the game takes me. I thought the graphics would be better than thy are but the game is still 5 stars.

i have been really enjoying this game, as an older player(64) have hit a few tuff spots but always found my way through it. I had almost 16 hours of game play and today it appears that there was an update to the game and now my load files are gone and the game wont let me start where I left off. it wants to me start a new game. as much as i have been enjoying the game im not sure that I like it enough to start from the beginning again.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your kind words and for spending so much time with the game — we really appreciate players like you who take the journey all the way through, even the tough spots. We’re truly sorry that your save data disappeared after the recent update. Losing 16 hours of progress is incredibly frustrating, and we completely understand why you wouldn’t want to start over.

We want to let you know that we’re actively working on a fix for the save/load issue that has affected some players. Our team is treating this as a top priority, and we’re doing everything we can to resolve it as quickly as possible.

Thank you again for your patience and for giving the game a chance. We hope to have this issue corrected soon

reviews.review_title: Worth the money. Needs Bhaptics support
reviews.review_description: Great game and graphics.  Well worth the money and I bought it at full price.  Difficult to start with at first but enjoyable. Only down side is no bhaptics support.  Would be perfect if it was added. Well done guys.

developer_response.body: where looking into it!

reviews.review_description: Easy to play

Every now and again a game comes out that shows off what a quest 3 can really do. This is one of them. The devs have worked hard and it shows.
Very immersive and fun. Great story too, highly recommend.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much, both for your feedback and your support! It means a lot to us!

reviews.review_description: Originally I had written a fairly negative review paired with 3 stars because of a dissapointing first experience on launch day. Since then though the game has updated. Now the graphics look great and feel way more immersive and up to date with other quest 3 games. After re-trying the game with the new graphics and bugs update I realized the game genuinely is fantastic now! I’m a few hours in and I’m loving it. Although my first experience wasn’t great, the latest update smoothed out pretty much all the problems I originally had with it and now i see that the game is actually a really fun, scary and entertaining experience that is 100% worth a try.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much Blockay, that means the world to us that you gave it another shot!

Feedback like yours really helps us make MEMOREUM the best it can be. We're glad you are enjoying your time aboard the Dawn now.

Thanks for the support!

reviews.review_description:
Great game fun to play but ￼ can not continue past core level two when I go into the break room and drop down the hole and go to the next area.  It reloads the screen that says core level one and just sits there and keeps loading.

￼ I have uninstalled the game and reinstalled it and still the same problem. 

￼￼ I have also checked for updates foe the game and everything has been updated and still the same problem.

I really like this game however it’s hard to leave a five star review when I can’t even complete it .


￼ ￼ So my wonderful  developers, if  you could help me out, that would be great.

I am playing on a quest3.

￼ hopefully, there will be a fix because I don’t feel like starting back over and going through the entire game to get to that point.

reviews.review_title: Door stuck closed in Executive Office
reviews.review_description: This game was by far the best game I’ve played on VR until…. I got to the executive office in the research center. It appears after watching others on YouTube that I should be able to insert two small tape looking things into a computer and a door will open however the computer is already closed when I enter the room. I cannot continue and it kills me I can’t finish the game.

developer_response.body:
I’m really sorry for the frustration this has caused. We’re aware of the save-data issue and the impact it’s having, and our team is working around the clock to get it fixed as quickly as possible. The workaround the developers provided is only temporary, and we know it isn’t idealespecially if you’re spawning in without weapons.

We appreciate your patience, and we’re doing everything we can to restore the game experience you were enjoying. Thank you for sticking with us.we wil add a chapter select soon

reviews.review_description:
Despite requesting a refund I'm rating this four stars because it seems like a pretty solid game from what I've played so far. People who feel the average shooter is too easy might really enjoy it.

Right from the first enemy encounter it's frustratingly difficult, even on the easiest difficulty setting. I didn't die during that encounter but it seemed like an extreme way to start the game and a bad sign if the difficulty gets higher during the course of the game.

The enemies move fast and are real bullet sponges. Reloading is slow and the initial weapon is severely underpowered.

The second weapon you find is more powerful but ammo for it is scarce.

I was staggering the enemies as the developer suggested in their response to another review, but it doesn't help much when you're out of ammo and back to the slow to reload pistol.

This game is only for the hardcore looking for an extreme challenge.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for giving the game a fair rating even after requesting a refund we really appreciate that. And don’t worry, you’re not “bad” at the game at all. Let me clarify how the balance is designed:

Easy difficulty is tailored to be a breezy, accessible experience.
Normal and Hard are meant to be pretty tough challenges, but Easy is intentionally forgiving so players can focus more on the story and atmosphere.

The first enemies are designed to drop after 12 pistol hits.
Your starting clip holds 8 shots, so yes  one reload is expected for that encounter. That pacing is intentional for the introductory fight.

The sidearm is supposed to feel weak because it has infinite ammo and functions as a fallback weapon rather than your main damage tool.

You’ve only scratched the surface of what the game offers in terms of weapon variety, upgrades, and mechanics. As the arsenal grows, combat flow becomes more dynamic and far less punishing.

We hope you’ll consider giving it another try down the line  and thank you again for your honest and thoughtful feedback.

reviews.review_description: I gave it a chance and was very pleasantly surprised. The graphics are amazing, the action, the tension, the shooting, everything is good. Once dude jumped on me in a spacesuit, broke the glass on his helmet and saw his face, pure horror. The only minus is that sometimes your hands get caught on objects.

developer_response.body:
Hi everyone! We wanted to give a quick update regarding the recent save/load issue that has affected some players. Our team is already working on a fix, and resolving this is our top priority. We truly appreciate your patience while we get everything back to normal.

We also want to say thank you to everyone who has been enjoying the game and supporting it. Your positive energy and feedback mean a lot to us. Ratings and reviews help keep the game visible and growing, and your support really does make a difference while we work through unexpected issues like this.

If you run into any problems, please reach out to us first  we’re here to help, and we can usually address issues quickly with your info. We want to make sure your experience stays fun and frustration-free.

Thank you again for playing and for sticking with us. We’re on it, and we’ll keep you updated!

reviews.review_description: I loved playing this game, my only issue is climbing ladders is so hard as the hands don't quite work as they go missing when you climb and the dismount is difficult, other than that the game runs smooth the graphics,music and atmosphere are great,sort the ladder climb then you have 5 stars

reviews.review_description:
Memoreum is a sci-fi survival horror game that takes place aboard a spaceship, carrying earth’s last survivors on a journey to a new planet to call home.
You play as Dr. Otto Hudson, who is awakened early out from stasis sleep, to find out all is not well aboard the ship. Without giving away too much of the story, you discover the ship’s crew has undergone changes, both physically and mentally. Now you must explore the ship, find supplies, unravel what has happened and survive the nightmare you have awakened into.

To survive this nightmare, you will have to search the ship for weapons and supplies and learn to manage your limited resources. The game also has a crafting feature which allows you to make valuable ammunition, health packs, upgrades and more. The game has three different difficult levels which not only makes the creatures more difficult to take down, but also changes the amount of the items scattered throughout the ship. Among the creatures lurking throughout the ship, you will also encounter six boss fights to overcome.

The game features a rich story that slowly unfolds, through finding messages/logs on the ships computers, personal data pads and audio logs scattered throughout the ship. The game also has three different ends to unlock, giving you a reason to play through more than once.
The game environment and characters look amazing and has enhanced graphics for the Quest 3 and Quest 3S. The corridors and rooms of the ship give you a cold sense of isolation, with a very creepy atmosphere that will make you approach everything slowly with a strong sense of fear. The developers really put time and thought into the design of this game and the final product shows that they have a love for this genre.

The sounds and music of the game fit the environment amazing, making the hairs on your arm stand and sending chills down your spine.  You will hear the sounds of the ship, creaking, hissing, and beeping, really placing you into the environment. You will also hear frightening things, things like unhuman breathing, footsteps and other terrifying sounds that will have you looking in all directions, trying to figure out where they are coming from. The game also has a rocking sounding track and 20 voice actors adding to the tension of the game.

The controls of the game are very straight forward and make great use of VR’s motion controls and allowing you to perform actions through movements and interactions. There are also all the comfort setting and options you will need to make the game fit for your play style. My only gripe with the controls is using the inventory system. Sometimes placing items into your storage pouches and retrieving them can be a pain. Items dropping to the ground instead of being stored and items being accidently grabbed from them when trying to do another task.

Overall this is an excellent sci-fi horror game that shows the developers grew up on these genres and have a deep love for them. If you like feeling; fear, suspense and jump scares this game is for you. It has all the elements that make this genre of game great.

reviews.review_title: The VR Horrors Persist, I’m Here For It
reviews.review_description:
A longer review is pending, but initial impressions are extremely promising for this well-designed narrative horror first-person shooter. Memoreum is great VR title worth your time and money. 

Known for their impressive Return to Rapture (Bioshock) HL: Alyx mod, Patient 8 Games is a small but talented team that has been hard at work on this title for the past 4 years and it shows. The display and controls are intuitive and immersive, designed specifically for VR. 

This game is the closest yet to capturing the feeling of Dead Space (but told from a virtual perspective), players are no longer safe behind a screen on their couch, they are up-close-and-personal with all the horrors this game has to offer. 

Not content with being just another monster shooter, the narrative compels you to press on and uncover the mystery surrounding what happened. The game even has three endings and plays in around 10-15 hours. I cannot wait to see what this developer makes next (my personal hope is that they get the opportunity to bring the Bioshock series into VR), support this game, you won’t regret it!

I wasn't sure I would enjoy this game.  But once you get immersed into the game, it is a lot of fun.  The mechanics of moving through the game are very smooth and feel natural.  I found the controls to be well layed out and functional.  The graphics are great.  The one area I found quirky was trying to climb ladders, or maybe that was just me.  I found the game challenging, but not so hard that you get frustrated and give up.  I would recommend this game.

reviews.review_description:
Just completed it. It took me 15 hours when the dev said it usually takes 10 hours. I must have been faffing about too much!

Overall, it's a great game! The story is great. Gun combat is great, and there are like 5 boss fights that are done really well. The first one was a bit clunky, but the others were good. I liked the use of tools in the game as well, which really added to it. Also, it's really small detail I noticed and really easy for players to miss, and I don't want to spoil anything, but I really liked what you did with the girls eyes near the end. It's a small detail, but it adds to the immersion of vr. Nice touch! 

The only thing that let's the game down is the silly sounds the zombie make and what they say, like "I got you" or "give us a taste." It's hard to take it seriously. A lot of indie vr games have these silly sounds not just this game but we need something like the clickers from The Last of Us or something just a bit more serious and different then the usual silly sounds. Also, it's a small thing but I didn't like the font say on the 'nanocharge' and a lot of the posters looked like AI art, (not saying they are they just looked a bit off).

But overall, it's a great indie game that is on par with the likes of 'The Living Remain', which I also enjoyed. I think a lot of the negative reviews are because people were expecting AAA because of the marketing and hype around it. It's not AAA. It's more AA, but it's one of the best AA VR games I have played sitting close with likes of 'The Living Remain'.

developer_response.body: thank you so mutch for the kind words
